description: Fix scaffold correctness bugs across state mutation, lifecycle hooks, SSE behavior, memo persistence, manifest metadata, and public state safety, and add focused regression coverage for the affected core modules.

userStories.title: Inspect canonical state mutation and rebuild path
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to identify the canonical state mutation flow and room rebuild dependency points so the scaffold fix can update state in the correct place without broad refactoring.
acceptanceCriteria: The code path used by POST /set_state and the canonical runtime mutation path are identified in repository notes or implementation comments.
The code path used to rebuild rooms from persisted agent state is identified.
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Documented the current canonical runtime mutation path in src/state.ts (`updateAgentState`), the compat `/set_state` divergence in src/ui-routes.ts, and the room rebuild dependency in src/index.ts.

userStories.title: Route set_state through canonical state mutation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need POST /set_state to use the canonical mutation path so persisted agent state and rebuilt room state remain consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /set_state updates agent state through the same mutation flow used by normal runtime updates.
Rebuilding a room after a state update reflects the latest persisted area value.
A focused regression test covers stale room rebuilding after POST /set_state.
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Compat POST /set_state now calls the same updateAgentState() flow used by AWN actions via a route dependency from src/index.ts, and a node:test regression verifies room membership rebuilds from the persisted state object after the route runs.

userStories.title: Separate idle eviction from voluntary leave in lifecycle logic
userStories.description: As a developer, I need lifecycle handling to distinguish idle eviction from voluntary leave so presence state and downstream events match actual agent behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Idle eviction leaves the agent present in state with online set to false.
Voluntary leave removes the agent from state.
Lifecycle logic differentiates the two paths without changing unrelated hook behavior.
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Align emitted presence events with eviction and leave behavior
userStories.description: As a developer, I need SSE-facing event emission to broadcast offline and leave events correctly so clients can react to idle eviction and voluntary leave distinctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Idle eviction emits agent_offline instead of agent_leave.
Voluntary leave still emits agent_leave.
A focused test asserts the emitted event names for both paths.
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Make post_memo resilient to persistence write failures
userStories.description: As a developer, I need post_memo to preserve in-memory success when persistence fails so action handlers remain available during filesystem issues.
acceptanceCriteria: post_memo updates in-memory memo state even when filesystem persistence fails.
A filesystem write failure during post_memo does not throw from the action handler.
post_memo returns an ok result when in-memory state was updated successfully.
A focused test covers the persistence failure scenario.
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Harden MemoStore startup and corrupt file loading
userStories.description: As a developer, I need MemoStore initialization to handle directory creation failures and corrupt memo files safely so startup and reload paths remain usable.
acceptanceCriteria: MemoStore construction does not throw when memo directory creation fails.
Corrupt memo files are skipped or handled without crashing memo loading.
Valid memo files remain readable when corrupt files are present.
Focused tests cover directory creation failure and corrupt memo file handling.
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Return deep-copied data from public state accessors
userStories.description: As a developer, I need getPublicState to return deep copies so callers cannot mutate internal state through shared nested object references.
acceptanceCriteria: getPublicState returns new nested object or array references instead of exposing source references.
Mutating a nested field on returned public state does not change internal source state.
A focused regression test covers nested mutation safety.
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Align manifest state_fields with public state contract
userStories.description: As a developer, I need manifest metadata to match the public state payload exactly so consumers and tooling can rely on the documented state contract.
acceptanceCriteria: Manifest state_fields exactly match the keys exposed by the public state payload.
Manifest state_fields include todayMemos and yesterdayMemo when those keys are present in public state.
No manifest state_fields entry remains for a key that is absent from public state.
A focused test validates manifest field accuracy.
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add focused regression coverage for hooks, SSE, and routes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need targeted integration tests across hooks, SSE, and UI routes so the offline, leave, manifest, and route-level memo behaviors stay locked down.
acceptanceCriteria: Hooks tests verify idle eviction marks an agent offline without removing it.
Hooks tests verify voluntary leave removes the agent and emits the leave event.
SSE or route-level tests verify agent_offline for idle eviction and agent_leave for voluntary leave.
UI route tests verify manifest state_fields match the public state payload contract.
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Run the standard test workflow with new core module coverage
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the standard project test command to run the new state, memo-store, hooks, SSE, and route regressions so the fixes are verified under the same workflow used by CI and contributors.
acceptanceCriteria: The project test command runs the new or updated state, hooks, SSE, memo-store, and UI route tests without extra manual steps.
All new and affected tests pass under the standard test command.
Any required test setup changes are committed in the repository.
Typecheck passes
